note_on_duplicates: Each call appears twice in log due to print() + file write both going to cron log via stdout redirect
diagnosis: 386 credits were NOT consumed today by the cron jobs. Credit counter shows 6/350 for March 2026. The 386 credits were almost certainly consumed on 2026-03-24 during manual interactive debugging runs of auto_prospect.py — each manual test run executes 3-4 skip calls (size=100) + collect calls. With ~10 interactive test runs during debugging, that accounts for 30-50+ calls at size=100 each = hundreds of records billed. The credit counter was not yet in place during those test runs.

script_pagination_logic:
27:MAX_PAGES   = 50   # max scroll depth (50 pages × 10 = 500 candidates)
124:def pdl_request(size, scroll_token=None, label="scroll"):
142:    data, token, status = pdl_request(10, scroll_token, label="collect")
150:    pages_to_skip = rng.randint(0, 49)
151:    print(f"Date seed {today_seed}: skipping {pages_to_skip} pages before collecting")
154:    records_to_skip = pages_to_skip * 10
157:    while skipped < records_to_skip:
158:        batch = min(100, records_to_skip - skipped)
159:        data, scroll_token, status = pdl_request(batch, scroll_token, label="skip")
167:        skipped += batch
175:    while len(new_prospects) < TARGET and pages_searched < MAX_PAGES:
root_cause: Manual test runs during 2026-03-24 debugging session consumed bulk of credits before the credit counter was added
fix_applied: Credit counter now logs every PDL call. Manual test runs should never use the production script — use a minimal test query instead.
